updates.summary: Bloom manages freight across parcel, LTL, FTL, and specialized channels with automatic rule-based routing. Shipment quotes and bookings are submitted via the shipment request form, with standard ground rates returned within 1 business day. Bookings require at least 1 business day advance notice. MSDS required for battery shipments. Business hours: 8AM-5PM EST, Mon-Fri.
updates.body:
Shipping hardware products is harder than shipping t-shirts. These things are heavy, awkward, sometimes hazardous (hello, lithium batteries), and customers still expect them to show up in one piece.

Bloom manages freight across four channels:

- Parcel — standard carrier shipping for parts, accessories, and lighter items
- LTL (Less Than Truckload) — individual units or small batches
- FTL (Full Truckload) — dedicated loads for big orders
- Specialized freight — white glove, crated, palletized, and roll-on/roll-off for oversized vehicles and equipment

Routing is automatic and rule-based. Shipments get classified by weight, dimensions, and product flags. If it's over 150 lbs or 108 inches in length, it bumps from parcel to LTL automatically. Products flagged as fragile, oversized, or hazmat route to specialized carriers. No manual guesswork, no billing disputes from wrong shipping classes.

On pricing: for small-format vehicles in loads of 5+, we've been hitting approximately $600/unit through volume consolidation and carrier optimization. Your numbers will vary depending on product and lanes.

Our logistics partner runs a curated carrier network classified by insurance levels, equipment type (floor tie-down capability matters for vehicles), and performance history.

## Submitting Shipment Requests

To request a shipping quote or booking, fill out the shipment request form, which can also be accessed through your company profile on the Bloom platform. Once your request has been processed you will receive an acknowledgement of your form submission along with an assigned shipment reference number via email.

## Quote Timeframes

- Standard ground shipments within the continental US: 1 business day
- Standard air, ocean, and/or international shipments: 3-5 business days
- Specialized trucking equipment or complex logistics projects: 3-7+ business days depending on scope; Bloom's shipping department will reach out directly to coordinate

Updates or edits to shipping quote submissions require additional processing time and may extend the timeframe for receiving your rates.

## Booking a Shipment

Booking requests must be submitted at least one business day in advance of the desired shipping date. Complex logistics projects requiring specialized equipment typically require bookings submitted at least 5 business days in advance. The Bloom operations team will coordinate with you directly for any specialized booking requests.

## Required Documents

- An MSDS must be on file to fulfill shipment requests for parcels containing batteries
- International shipment requests must be accompanied by a commercial invoice
- Missing or incomplete documentation may extend the lead time for booking confirmations and/or quote returns

## Business Hours

Bloom offices are staffed between 8AM-5PM EST Monday through Friday. Shipping requests sent after 3:30PM EST may not be processed until the next business day.

updates.summary: Bloom offers final mile delivery at four service levels: Standard (appointment + curbside), Premium (threshold, unpacking, debris removal), Premium+Assembly (up to 30 min light assembly), and Roll-on/Roll-off (no packaging). Individual delivery upgrades available including appointment scheduling, threshold delivery, unpacking, debris removal, and room of choice. Not all upgrades available from every carrier.
updates.body:
Final mile is the last leg. Product goes from a regional hub or carrier terminal to the customer's door. It's usually the most expensive per-mile segment and where the most things go wrong.

## Service Levels

To ensure all customers receive best-in-class service tailored to their specific needs, Bloom offers the following service level options:

- Standard — delivery by appointment, drop-off at curb or driveway
- Premium — standard plus over threshold/inside delivery, unpacking, debris removal
- Premium+Assembly — premium plus up to 30 minutes of light assembly tasks such as installing side-view mirrors or screwing on small furniture legs; tasks must not require use of tools and does not include PDI or Quality Control checklists
- Roll-on/Roll-off (RO/RO) — items are shipped fully assembled and without any kind of packaging and/or palletization from point of pickup to point of delivery

## Individual Delivery Upgrades

Deliveries can also be completely customized by selecting upgrades on an individual basis:

- Delivery by Appointment — the recipient will be contacted to schedule their delivery date and time in advance
- Beyond Threshold (Over Threshold) — item is delivered packaged and/or palletized into the first secure and dry area such as a garage or just inside the front door
- Unpacking — items are shipped packaged and/or palletized and are unpacked at the delivery site
- Debris Removal — typically paired with unpacking services; all packaging debris is removed by the carrier at point of delivery
- Room of Choice — item is delivered packaged and/or palletized into a specific room as instructed by the recipient

Not all upgrades are available from every carrier. Some upgrades such as room-of-choice or assembly time may limit the carrier pool.

## Coverage

Coverage is handled through a mix of national carriers with local delivery arms and regional last-mile specialists. Not every service level is available in every metro. We'll tell you upfront what's possible for your product and geography.

## Roll-on/Roll-off Delivery

One thing specific to electric mobility: roll-on/roll-off delivery (sometimes called naked delivery or no-box). The vehicle ships fully assembled with no packaging. Driver rolls it off the truck. Lower shipping cost, lower waste, but requires carriers with the right equipment and insurance.

articles.title: Battery Shipment Guidelines
articles.summary: Lithium-ion batteries are Class 9 hazardous materials requiring MSDS documentation and UN number classification. Key UN numbers: 3480 (standalone batteries), 3481 (packed with/in equipment), 3556 (lithium-ion battery-powered vehicles). UN 3556 replaced UN 3171 as of January 2025. MSDS must list all applicable UN codes.
articles.body:
Batteries are regulated as dangerous goods due to their potential to cause fires, explosions, and other hazards if damaged or mishandled during transit. Within the US, batteries are shipped as Class 9 (miscellaneous) hazardous materials and require certain types of documentation and/or parcel markings based on how they are being shipped.

The US Dept. of Transportation and the Federal Aviation Administration both have useful online resources for battery shippers, though documentation may be out of date regarding the 2025 regulation changes described below.

## UN Numbers

UN numbers are 4-digit global codes that help emergency responders and regulatory bodies quickly assess potential risks and responses to dangerous goods shipments. UN codes for dangerous goods must be listed on shipping documents along with 24-hour availability contact information for emergencies (typically the shipper or a specialized DG hotline service).

The primary UN numbers used for shipments containing lithium-ion batteries are:

- UN 3480 (Lithium Ion Batteries) — lithium batteries shipped as standalone items, not with or inside equipment
- UN 3481 (Lithium ion batteries packed with or contained in equipment) — batteries that are pre-installed in equipment, or batteries contained within their own packaging that is then packed alongside equipment and secured within the outer container
- UN 3556 (Vehicle, lithium-ion battery powered) — specific code for vehicles shipped with lithium-ion batteries pre-installed

## 2025 Regulation Update

UN 3556 replaced the previous classification UN 3171 (Battery-powered vehicle), which did not delineate between vehicles shipped with different types of batteries. Updated codes UN 3556, 3557, and 3558 specifying battery types went into effect January 1, 2025.

- DOT does not yet have the revised 2025 regulations on their website, but the International Air Transport Association (IATA) does
- MSDS documents with UN 3171 codes will need to be updated to reflect the new classifications

## MSDS Documentation

Shipments containing batteries must be accompanied by a Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) which outlines potential hazards, safe handling/transport guidelines, and emergency response procedures.

- The MSDS must list the UN codes associated with shipping that type of battery
- Multiple UN codes should be listed if the battery might be shipped in multiple ways
- Example: Batteries that might be shipped as standalone items, as extras packed alongside an electric vehicle, or pre-installed in a vehicle prior to shipping should have UN 3480, UN 3481, and UN 3556 all listed on their MSDS

articles.title: International Shipping Documents
articles.summary: International shipments require a commercial invoice (with incoterms, HTS codes, country of origin, broker info), bill of lading, packing list, and EEI/SED for shipments over $2,500. Battery shipments need MSDS/DG forms. Canada shipments require a BN9 from CRA and CARM registration. Temporary imports can use ATA Carnet to avoid customs fees.
articles.body:
International shipments require specific documentation to clear customs and comply with trade regulations. Missing or incomplete documentation may delay your shipment.

## Required Documents

- Commercial Invoice (aka Pro Forma Invoice) — the primary customs document with several high-priority fields detailed below
- Bill of Lading — typically issued after shipment is booked
- Packing List — supports the commercial invoice; must contain inventory and description of goods, sender/recipient info, shipment details (weights and dimensions), origin and destination
- Battery MSDS/DG Forms — additional documents required for shipments containing batteries or other dangerous goods
- Electronic Export Filing (EEI), aka Shipper's Export Declaration (SED) — required for international shipments valued over $2,500 or requiring a US export license; typically filed by the freight broker. Exception: freight shipments from the US into Canada
- Country-specific requirements — some countries may require additional documentation such as a Certificate of Origin

## Commercial Invoice Key Fields

- Terms of Sale (Incoterms) — define the responsibilities of sellers and buyers for international transactions. Bloom and/or your service provider does not assume responsibility for any costs or risks associated with international shipments; Incoterms must reflect either buyer or seller responsibility
- Harmonized Tariff Number (HTS code) — the number assigned to a specific item used to calculate import duties and fees
- Country of Origin/Manufacture — the country in which the item was produced; may differ from the country the item is being shipped from
- Broker Information — at a minimum, broker's name and telephone number. If you do not have a customs broker, we can arrange for the carrier to act as broker on your behalf; additional forms (POA) will be required

## Customs and Duty Fees

Fees can generally be quoted by the carrier company if provided with the shipment's Incoterms and HTS codes for all goods being shipped.

## Temporary Import/Export

Commercial goods can be imported temporarily — such as for use in trade shows or demos — without paying customs and duty fees by filing for an ATA Carnet. Carnet fees are determined by the value of goods being imported/exported.

## Shipping into Canada

Companies shipping goods into Canada must have a 9-digit business number (BN9) assigned by the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA). The Canadian Border Services Agency (CBSA) also requires registration in the CBSA Assessment and Revenue Management (CARM) system.

- Resident businesses can obtain a BN9 while registering in the CARM portal
- Non-resident businesses must get a BN9 directly from the CRA before registering in the CARM portal

articles.title: Customs Brokerage Services
articles.summary: CLN Worldwide provides standalone customs brokerage services for international shipments. Onboarding requires a Power of Attorney form, CBP Automated Clearinghouse enrollment (ACH# issued within 5-7 business days), and a CLN onboarding meeting. CLN handles customs clearance and ISF for ocean imports and air shipments with different document requirements for each mode.
articles.body:
CLN Worldwide is a logistics provider who can provide standalone customs brokerage services for international shipments. Although CLN also provides carrier services, they can provide standalone customs brokerage for shipments booked through other carriers.

## Onboarding Process

Customers who would like to sign up with CLN must complete the following:

- Complete and return CLN's Power of Attorney (POA) form
- Complete and return the application for US Customs and Border Patrol's Automated Clearinghouse (ACH) program, if not already enrolled. New applicants will receive an ACH number from CBP within 5-7 business days. The CBP ACH number must be reported to CLN for processing
- Complete an onboarding meeting with CLN representatives

The CBP ACH program is only for US Customs imports. Customs, duties, and fees associated with other international shipments will be paid by the CLN agent office and included on the customer's invoice for that shipment.

## Ocean Shipments

For US ocean imports, CLN can handle both customs clearance and ISF (10+2). The Importer Security Filing (ISF 10+2) must be filed at least 24 hours prior to vessel loading. To meet this requirement, ISF data must be provided before cargo is loaded at origin. The final Bill of Lading is also required prior to arrival for customs clearance.

Pre-departure documents are preferred so CLN can review compliance and complete filings on time.

Documents required for ocean shipments:

- ISF Form — must include HTS codes (6-digit minimum). If ISF is filed by another party, only confirmation and ISF transaction number is required
- Commercial Invoice — must include HTS codes (10-digit minimum) and product country of origin/manufacture
- Packing List
- Bill of Lading (once issued by carrier)
- Importer of Record details
- Any applicable permits, licenses, or PGA requirements
- Arrival notice from forwarder/carrier (typically received 5 days prior to arrival)

## Air Shipments

Documents should be sent prior to departure or immediately after booking. ISF forms are not required for air shipments. The final Air Waybill (AWB) is required before arrival to ensure timely clearance.

Documents required for air shipments:

- Commercial Invoice — must include HTS codes (10-digit minimum) and product country of origin/manufacture
- Packing List
- Air Waybill (once issued by carrier)
- Importer of Record details
- Any applicable permits or licenses
- Arrival notice from forwarder/carrier (typically received on day of arrival)
